methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terran Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zach Dwiel and Daniel Weddle. The valuation universe consists of 9 comparable companies: 1 public peer (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private robotics firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The robotics and manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but the filtered peer set—restricted to same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier companies—yields fewer than 3 datapoints, the minimum threshold for reliable median estimation. A broad sector median would introduce unacceptable selection bias given the wide dispersion in company maturity, revenue, and capital efficiency across the robotics market.

Without a sufficiently robust peer-median result, this valuation remains incomplete pending alternative methods such as venture capital method analysis, discounted cash flow modeling, or expansion of the comparable company set through adjacent sector peers. The identified public and private comparables provide a directional frame but insufficient statistical weight for a point estimate or range. Further work should either narrow the definition of comparability through business-model filtering or apply cash-flow-based techniques to establish a defensible valuation range.
